# Opal Privacy Manifest

**Branch: `v2`.** Generated by a hand audit of the source tree on 2026-05-19. Sources are
quoted by file:line so anyone can re-verify.

Opal does **NOT** phone home. There is no opt-in telemetry, no crash reporter, no
analytics SDK, no third-party JS, no `metrics.opal.com`, no anonymized "ping". Every
outbound network call below is one the user opted into by using a feature.

This document enumerates **every** endpoint the binary may contact, sorted by category.
Entries marked **[v2]** describe hardening that has shipped or is shipping on this branch
(see `docs/V2-PANEL.md` and `docs/V2-ROADMAP.md`).

If you find an outbound call that is not in this list, please file a bug.

---

## Always-on (fires without an explicit user action)

These run during `appInit()` / first frame, before the user opts into anything specific.
They are listed first so they get scrutiny. Each is a **priority item to make opt-in** in
the next sprint (tracked in `V2-ROADMAP.md` Wave 1).

| Endpoint | Trigger | Request payload | PII | Toggle |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `https://api.github.com/repos/debpalash/Opal/releases/latest` | `appInit` background thread fires `updater.checkAsync()` on every launch (`src/main.zig:144`) | GET, `Accept: application/vnd.github+json`, `User-Agent: Opal-Updater`. No body. (`src/services/updater.zig:103-130`) | None besides IP + UA | No UI toggle today. Hardcoded fire-on-launch. **Flag: should be opt-in.** |
| `https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/releases/latest/download/yt-dlp_macos` (or `…/yt-dlp` on Linux) | `appInit` calls `ytdlp.ensureAvailable()` (`src/main.zig:140`). Downloads only if `~/.config/zigzag/bin/yt-dlp` is missing. | GET, no auth, redirected via `curl -L`. (`src/services/ytdlp.zig:69-115`) | None besides IP | No UI toggle. Skipped if the binary already exists. **Flag: should at minimum show a "fetch yt-dlp from GitHub?" prompt.** |

Everything else in this document is user-initiated.

---

## User-initiated features

### Metadata — TMDB
- **Hosts:** `api.themoviedb.org`, `image.tmdb.org`
- **Trigger:** User opens the TMDB drawer / searches / scrolls a list. Requires the user
  to paste a TMDB v4 bearer token into Settings → General; without it the entire feature
  is dark (`src/services/tmdb.zig:26-29`, "TMDB API Key Required" empty state).
- **URL patterns** (`src/services/tmdb_api.zig:107-138`):
  - `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/search/{multi|movie|tv}?query=<URL-encoded>&page=N`
  - `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/trending/{type}/{day|week}?page=N`
  - `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/{movie|tv}/popular?page=N`
  - `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/movie/now_playing?page=N` / `…/upcoming`
  - `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/discover/movie?with_genres={id}&sort_by=popularity.desc&page=1`
  - Resolver also hits `https://api.themoviedb.org/3/search/multi?...` and
    `…/3/{movie|tv}/{id}/external_ids?...` to map a title to an IMDB ID for Stremio
    add-ons (`src/services/resolver.zig:944-989`). Also used by the AI "search TMDB"
    tool (`src/services/ai_tools.zig:412`).
- **Auth:** Bearer token in `Authorization` header.
- **Request body:** None. URL parameters only (search query + page).
- **PII:** The search query (whatever the user types) and your IP. No login, no
  per-account history.
- **Posters:** `https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/w185{poster_path}` fetched per item once on
  scroll (`src/services/tmdb_api.zig:166`). No auth.
- **Toggle:** Don't paste a key → feature stays off. There is no setting labelled
  "disable TMDB"; absence of the key is the toggle.

### Subtitles — OpenSubtitles
Two distinct endpoints in two distinct code paths.

#### Modern: `api.opensubtitles.com/api/v1` (`src/services/subtitles.zig`)
- **Trigger:** User opens the Subtitles drawer and clicks Search / Download.
- **URL pattern:** `https://api.opensubtitles.com/api/v1/subtitles?query=<…>&languages=<…>`
  (search) and `…/api/v1/download` (POST `{"file_id":N}`).
- **Auth:** `Api-Key: <user's key>` header. Without a key the feature errors with
  "Set OpenSubtitles API key in Settings" (`src/services/subtitles.zig:64-66`).
- **PII:** the cleaned media filename / IMDB ID, your IP, and your OpenSubtitles API
  key (which OpenSubtitles uses to identify the requesting user).
- **Toggle:** Don't paste a key.

#### Legacy: `rest.opensubtitles.org` (`src/player/subtitles.zig`)
- **Trigger:** Native subtitle engine, invoked when auto-fetching subs for the currently
  playing file.
- **URL pattern:**
  `https://rest.opensubtitles.org/search/query-<query>/sublanguageid-<lang>`
  (`src/player/subtitles.zig:178`).
- **Auth:** `User-Agent: Opal/1.0`. No API key. (This is the legacy REST endpoint.)
- **PII:** cleaned filename + IP.
- **Toggle:** Only fires when the user invokes auto-subs in the player.

### YouTube
Two backends, tried in order.

- **Piped instances (primary):** GET `https://{instance}/search?q=<URL-encoded>&filter=videos`
  iterating `pipedapi.kavin.rocks`, `pipedapi.adminforge.de`, `api.piped.yt`
  (`src/services/youtube.zig:15-83`). No auth. Sends search query + IP.
- **yt-dlp (fallback):** `yt-dlp ytsearch10:<query>` subprocess — yt-dlp contacts
  `youtube.com` directly. (`src/services/youtube.zig:172-184`)
- **Thumbnails:** `https://i.ytimg.com/vi/<id>/mqdefault.jpg` per result (`youtube.zig:157`).
- **Direct watch URL:** `https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=<id>` loaded into mpv — mpv uses
  ytdl-hook / yt-dlp to fetch the manifest.
- **Resolver also calls yt-dlp** `ytsearch5:<query>` (`src/services/resolver.zig:870`).
- **Trigger:** User searches in the YouTube tab, or types a YT URL.
- **PII:** search query, IP, and whatever yt-dlp negotiates with Google.
- **Toggle:** Don't use the YouTube tab. yt-dlp is a system binary; if a user wants
  zero contact with Google, they should not enter youtube URLs.

### Jellyfin (user's own server, but still outbound)
- **Host:** Whatever the user types into Settings → Jellyfin → Server URL. Could be
  `http://192.168.1.10:8096` (LAN) or a public hostname.
- **URL patterns** (`src/services/jellyfin.zig`):
  - `POST <server>/Users/AuthenticateByName` with body
    `{"Username":"…","Pw":"…"}` (`:46-52`)
  - `GET <server>/Videos/<itemId>/stream?static=true&api_key=<token>` (`:343`)
  - `GET <server>/Audio/<itemId>/universal?api_key=<token>&UserId=<uid>` (`:360`)
  - `GET <server>/Items/<itemId>/Images/Primary?maxWidth=200&quality=80&api_key=<token>` (`:409`)
- **Auth:** `X-Emby-Authorization: MediaBrowser Client="ZigZag", Device="Desktop", DeviceId="zigzag-001", Version="1.0"` (`:55`).
- **PII:** Username and password sent in cleartext POST body (will be TLS if the user's
  server URL is `https://`); `DeviceId` is hardcoded to `zigzag-001` (same for all
  Opal users) — flag this as a fingerprinting concern but it's intentional for
  device-list dedup on Jellyfin's side.
- **Toggle:** Empty the Jellyfin server URL field.

### Stremio add-ons
- **Catalog fetch:** `GET https://stremio-addons.com/catalog.json`
  (`src/services/stremio.zig:56`) — triggered when user opens the Stremio drawer.
- **Add-on manifests / streams:** whichever URL the add-on was installed with — typical
  defaults from `addKnownAddons` (`:135-142`):
  - `https://torrentio.strem.fun/manifest.json` (Torrentio)
  - `https://cyberflix.elfhosted.com/manifest.json`
  - `https://mediafusion.elfhosted.com/manifest.json`
  - `https://comet.elfhosted.com/manifest.json`
  - `https://anime-kitsu.strem.fun/manifest.json`
  - `https://opensubtitles-v3.strem.io/manifest.json`
- **Stream queries:** `GET <addon_base>/stream/{movie|series}/<imdb_id>.json`
  (`src/services/resolver.zig:1024-1027`).
- **PII:** The IMDB ID of what you're trying to watch (resolved via TMDB upstream)
  flows to **every installed add-on at once**. That's by design of the Stremio
  protocol, but worth knowing.
- **Toggle:** Don't install any add-ons.

### Comics — readallcomics.com
- **URL:** `https://readallcomics.com/?story=<query>&s=&type=comic` (search,
  `src/services/comics.zig:374`) plus arbitrary issue-page URLs the user clicks.
  Image fetches go to whichever `*.bp.blogspot.com` host the page references.
- **Trigger:** User opens the Comics tab and clicks a comic.
- **Method:** curl subprocess with browser-shaped `User-Agent`
  (`comics.zig:69-74`).
- **PII:** Search query + IP.

### ProjectJav scraper
- **Trigger:** Only when the user types a URL containing `projectjav.com/movie/`
  (`src/services/projectjav.zig:50-52`).
- **Method:** curl with Chrome `User-Agent` to fetch the page HTML, then parsing
  magnet links locally.
- **PII:** Whatever URL the user pasted.

### AnimePahe (anime DDL fallback)
- **URLs:**
  - `https://animepahe.pw/api?m=search&q=<…>` (`src/services/anime.zig:498`)
  - `https://animepahe.pw/play/<session>/<episode>` loaded into mpv (`:537`)
- **Headers:** `Referer: https://animepahe.pw`, Firefox `User-Agent`.
- **Trigger:** Anime playback when allanime isn't available.

### Jina Reader (AI "read URL" tool)
- **URL:** `https://r.jina.ai/<encoded URL>` (`src/services/ai_tools.zig:501`)
- **Trigger:** The local Gemma / apfel LLM calls the `read_webpage` tool. Local-IP
  URLs are blocked (`:492-497`).
- **PII:** Whatever URL the AI is asked to read.
- **Toggle:** Don't use the AI chat, or don't ask it to read URLs.

### MPV-script downloads (Settings → Scripts → Install)
User clicks an "Install" button next to a recommended script
(`src/services/scripts.zig:157-182`):
- `https://codeberg.org/jouni/mpv_sponsorblock_minimal/raw/branch/master/sponsorblock_minimal.lua`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mpv-player/mpv/master/TOOLS/lua/autoload.lua`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/christoph-heinrich/mpv-quality-menu/master/quality-menu.lua`
- `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/po5/thumbfast/master/thumbfast.lua`

Fetched via curl. One-shot per install. **Sponsorblock itself talks to its own server
when active inside mpv** — that's the script's behaviour, not Opal's, but worth
mentioning.

### RSS feeds
- **Default seed:** `https://myrss.org/eztv` (`src/services/rss.zig:56`) — added by
  `init()` on first launch.
- **Custom feeds:** Whatever URL the user adds.
- **Trigger:** User opens RSS drawer or hits Refresh.
- **Method:** curl GET.

### EZTV direct-API supplement
On every torrent search where the engine filter is `all` or `eztv`
(`src/services/search.zig:265-266`):
- `GET https://eztvx.to/api/get-torrents?limit=100&page=1`
- Headers: `Accept: application/json`, `User-Agent: ZigZag/1.0`
- Client-side filters by the search query.

### Anime — Jikan / allanime
- **Jikan (MyAnimeList community API):**
  - `https://api.jikan.moe/v4/top/anime?filter=airing&limit=24` (trending,
    `src/services/anime.zig:42`)
  - `https://api.jikan.moe/v4/anime?q=<query>&limit=24` (search, `:83`)
  - `https://api.jikan.moe/v4/anime/<mal_id>/episodes?page=N` (episode list, `:316`)
- **allanime GraphQL:**
  - `https://api.allanime.day/api?variables=<…>&query=<…>` (resolver,
    `src/services/resolver.zig:806`, and player flow)
  - `Referer: https://allmanga.to` (`:812`)
- **Trigger:** Anime tab open + browse / search / pick episode.

### Live-stream resolution — Streamlink
- **Trigger:** User opens a URL whose host is in the streamlink list (Twitch, Kick,
  Crunchyroll, Chaturbate, Pluto.tv, etc. — full list at
  `src/services/streamlink.zig:13-33`).
- **Outbound:** Streamlink, a Python library invoked via
  `streamlink_resolve.py`, contacts the named live-stream site directly to
  negotiate an HLS URL. Opal itself does not call those sites — `streamlink` does
  on Opal's behalf.

### YouTube playlist / channel extraction
`src/services/extractors.zig:94-107` shells out to `yt-dlp --flat-playlist -j …`,
optionally with `--proxy <user-proxy>`. yt-dlp talks to whatever site the URL is for.
**Privacy note:** the extractor unconditionally passes
`--cookies-from-browser firefox`. This means **Opal will read your Firefox cookie jar
and ship the matching cookies to whatever host yt-dlp contacts** for any URL that
needs auth. The flow is local-to-yt-dlp (no detour through Opal's process), but it's
worth knowing.

---

## Optional sync services (require explicit OAuth / API key)

> **Status note:** As of this commit, the modules below contain wired-up code but
> **the call sites are not invoked anywhere in the player loop yet** (grep for
> `scrobbleStart`, `simkl.checkin`, `anilist.updateProgress` against the rest of
> `src/`). They are dormant — no traffic leaves the machine until/unless a future
> commit wires them into the playback events. Listed here so you know what they
> *will* send when enabled, per V2-ROADMAP Wave 3 (unified scrobble bus).

### Trakt
- **Host:** `https://api.trakt.tv` (`src/services/trakt.zig:11`).
- **Endpoints:** `/scrobble/start`, `/scrobble/pause`, `/scrobble/stop`.
- **Method:** POST via curl with `Content-Type: application/json`,
  `trakt-api-version: 2`, `trakt-api-key: <client_id>`,
  `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.
- **Payload:** `{"movie":{"title":"<title>"},"progress":<float>}`.
- **PII:** Whatever you're watching (movie / show title) + progress fraction +
  your OAuth identity on Trakt.
- **Toggle:** `enabled` flag in module state; user fills client_id +
  access_token in Settings.

### AniList
- **Host:** `https://graphql.anilist.co` (`src/services/anilist.zig:10`).
- **Method:** POST via curl with GraphQL `mutation { SaveMediaListEntry(...) }` body.
- **PII:** AniList media ID + episode number + your OAuth identity.
- **Toggle:** `enabled` flag + user pastes a personal access token.

### SIMKL
- **Host:** `https://api.simkl.com` (`src/services/simkl.zig:11`).
- **Endpoints:** `/sync/history`, `/sync/add-to-list`.
- **Method:** POST via curl with `simkl-api-key` + Bearer headers.
- **PII:** Titles + your SIMKL identity.
- **Toggle:** API key + access token in Settings.

---

## Indexers and scrapers (torrents)

Triggered **only** by an explicit search drawer query. Many flow through the bundled
Python engine harness (`engines/nova2.py` + `engines/engines/*.py`) which Opal spawns
as a subprocess (`src/services/search.zig:214-224`).

| Engine | URL pattern | Source |
|---|---|---|
| 1337x | `https://1337x.to/search/<query>/1/` + per-result `https://1337x.to/<detail>` | `src/services/resolver.zig:578,639` (direct) and `engines/engines/one337x.py` |
| YTS | `https://yts.mx/api/v2/list_movies.json?query_term=<…>&limit=8&sort_by=seeds` | `src/services/resolver.zig:709`; also `engines/engines/yts.py` |
| ThePirateBay | `https://apibay.org/q.php?…` (PirateBay relays via Apibay) | `engines/engines/piratebay.py:75-85` |
| Apibay | `https://apibay.org/q.php?q=<…>&cat=<…>` | `engines/engines/apibay.py:14,46` |
| UIndex | `https://uindex.org/search.php?search=<…>` | `engines/engines/uindex.py:17,60-61` |
| EZTV (scraper) | `https://eztvx.to/search/<query>` | `engines/engines/eztv.py:20,90` |
| EZTV (API) | `https://eztvx.to/api/get-torrents?limit=100&page=1` | `src/services/search.zig:283` |
| Nyaa | `https://nyaa.si/?<params>` | `engines/engines/nyaa.py:12,44` |
| TorrentProject | `https://torrentproject.com.se/browse?t=<…>&p=<…>` | `engines/engines/torrentproject.py:15,115` |
| LimeTorrents | `https://www.limetorrent.in/search/<cat>/<query>/seeds/<page>/` | `engines/engines/limetorrents.py:16,130` |
| SolidTorrents | `https://solidtorrents.to/...` | `engines/engines/solidtorrents.py:32` |
| TorrentsCSV | `https://torrents-csv.com/service/search?size=100&q=<…>` | `engines/engines/torrentscsv.py:37,57` |
| Torlock | `https://www.torlock.com/<cat>/torrents/<query>.html?sort=seeds&page=<n>` | `engines/engines/torlock.py:14,106` |
| KickAss (placeholder) | (file is a `404: Not Found` literal) | `engines/engines/kickass.py` — currently dead |

**PII for all of the above:** the search query (plus IP + a desktop-Firefox-shaped
`User-Agent`). No cookies, no fingerprint hash.

**Throttling [v2]:** Calls are gated by a shared per-origin token bucket in
`src/core/rate_limit.zig` (proof-of-life integration on 1337x; rollout to every
backend is V2-ROADMAP Wave 1 item 3).

---

## BitTorrent itself
Once you actually start a download / stream, libtorrent (linked via
`src/torrent_wrapper.cpp`) joins the BitTorrent swarm:

- **DHT bootstrap nodes** (`src/torrent_wrapper.cpp:117-121`):
  - `dht.transmissionbt.com:6881`
  - `router.bittorrent.com:6881`
  - `router.utorrent.com:6881`
  - `dht.aelitis.com:6881`
- **Default trackers added to every torrent** (`src/torrent_wrapper.cpp:163-179`):
  - `udp://tracker.opentrackr.org:1337/announce`
  - `udp://open.stealth.si:80/announce`
  - `udp://tracker.torrent.eu.org:451/announce`
  - `udp://tracker.bittor.pw:1337/announce`
  - `udp://public.popcorn-tracker.org:6969/announce`
  - `udp://tracker.dler.org:6969/announce`
  - `udp://exodus.desync.com:6969/announce`
  - `udp://open.demonii.com:1337/announce`
- Plus whatever trackers are encoded in the magnet/torrent file the user loaded.

**PII:** Your IP becomes visible to every peer in the swarm and every tracker.
That's intrinsic to BitTorrent, but is the single most "leaky" thing Opal does —
worth saying loud.

---

## Model / dependency downloads (lazy, opt-in via Settings)

`src/core/deps.zig` and `src/services/ai_server.zig` define explicit per-feature
"fetch this model" buttons. None fire automatically; each requires a Settings click.

| Asset | URL | Fired from |
|---|---|---|
| Sherpa Whisper-tiny ASR (~40 MB) | `https://github.com/k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx/releases/download/asr-models/sherpa-onnx-whisper-tiny.en.tar.bz2` | `deps.zig:140` |
| Sherpa Piper-VITS TTS (~40 MB) | `https://github.com/k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx/releases/download/tts-models/vits-piper-en_US-lessac-medium.tar.bz2` | `deps.zig:204` |
| Sherpa Kokoro TTS (~330 MB) | `https://github.com/k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx/releases/download/tts-models/kokoro-en-v0_19.tar.bz2` | `deps.zig:266` |
| Sherpa streaming Zipformer (~80 MB) | `https://github.com/k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx/releases/download/asr-models/sherpa-onnx-streaming-zipformer-en-2023-06-26.tar.bz2` | `deps.zig:324` |
| whisper.cpp tiny.en GGML (~39 MB) | `https://huggingface.co/ggerganov/whisper.cpp/resolve/main/ggml-tiny.en.bin` | `deps.zig:377` |
| MLX Whisper large-v3-turbo config | `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo/resolve/main/config.json` | `deps.zig:593` |
| MLX Whisper large-v3-turbo weights (~1.6 GB) | `https://huggingface.co/mlx-community/whisper-large-v3-turbo/resolve/main/weights.safetensors` | `deps.zig:604` |
| `uv` Python package manager installer | `https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh` piped to `sh` | `deps.zig:491` |
| Gemma 4 E2B GGUF (~3.17 GB) — local LLM | `https://huggingface.co/unsloth/gemma-4-E2B-it-GGUF/resolve/main/gemma-4-E2B-it-UD-Q4_K_XL.gguf` | `ai_server.zig:26` |
| Shimmy llama-server binary (Linux) | `https://github.com/Michael-A-Kuykendall/shimmy/releases/latest/download/shimmy-linux-x86_64` | `ai_server.zig:584` |

All requests are unauthenticated `curl`. The only data sent is your IP + UA — there
is no "tell HF which user is downloading". HuggingFace will log the download server-side,
as will GitHub.

---

## Local-only (NOT outbound, but listed so readers can audit)

These all bind to loopback unless noted.

- **Stream proxy** — `http://127.0.0.1:<dynamic port 45678–45777>/s/<16-hex-token>/...`
  (`src/player/stream_proxy.zig:31-33`). One listener per active torrent stream.
  Every URL contains a per-stream random token gated by constant-time compare —
  **a browser tab on the same host that doesn't know the token cannot fetch**.
  This closes the wildcard-CORS hole that existed before v2. **[v2 shipped]**
- **Watch-party** — listens on `0.0.0.0:41596` only when the user clicks "Host party"
  (`src/services/watch_party.zig:12,38-47`). LAN-exposed by design (it's for friends
  on your LAN). No auth on the protocol. **Flag: explicit LAN exposure when hosting.**
- **AI llama-server** — `http://127.0.0.1:41592` (`src/services/ai_server.zig:113`).
- **AI embedding server** — `http://127.0.0.1:41593` (`src/services/ai_memory.zig:60`).
- **Language-learning server (`lang_server.py`)** — `http://127.0.0.1:41594`
  (`src/services/lang_learn.zig:16,639`).
- **Voice backend `speaches`** — `http://localhost:8000` by default, overridable via
  `SPEACHES_URL` env var (`src/services/voice_backend.zig:363`).
- **mpv IPC socket** — Unix-domain socket per player. Not networked.

### Remote JSON API — `services/remote.zig`
- **Bind address:** `0.0.0.0:41595` — **the entire LAN by default**, not just
  loopback (`src/services/remote.zig:194-199`).
- **Auth status [v2]:** A per-launch bearer token is generated on first launch and
  persisted to `~/.config/zigzag/api.token` (mode `0600`); the server requires
  `Authorization: Bearer <hex>` and uses constant-time compare to avoid timing
  oracles (`remote.zig:20-128, 132-176`). This is V2-PANEL directive 7 and is
  landing in a parallel task ("Wave 1 #1" in `docs/V2-ROADMAP.md`).
- **Flag:** Because the bind is still `0.0.0.0`, any device on the same LAN that
  obtains the token (e.g. by reading it from a shared filesystem, or because the
  user pasted it into a web UI on another machine) can drive playback. Consider
  binding to `127.0.0.1` by default + a Settings opt-in for LAN exposure.

---

## What we DON'T send

- **No anonymous usage stats.** There is no `metrics.zigzag.app`. There is no
  `pendo`, `mixpanel`, `posthog`, `sentry`, or `bugsnag` import. (Grep the tree.)
- **No error / crash reports.** Logs go to a local ring buffer
  (`src/core/logs.zig`) visible only in the in-app Logs tab.
- **No "anonymized" anything.** There is no client UUID written anywhere outside
  of the Jellyfin `DeviceId="zigzag-001"` literal (which is hardcoded the same for
  every user, so it's the opposite of fingerprinting — Jellyfin can't distinguish
  installs from each other on that field alone).
- **No third-party SDKs.** Opal links libtorrent, mpv, sqlite, onnxruntime,
  sqlite-vec, SDL2 — that's it. No advertising or analytics libraries.
- **No automatic submission of search queries to a central server.** Each torrent
  search hits exactly the indexers the user enabled.

---

## Verification

This manifest is generated **by hand** from a tree-wide audit. The
methodology was:

1. `grep -rEn 'https?://' src/` (excluding `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`/`0.0.0.0`).
2. Trace every match back to the function that calls `http.fetch()`, `std.http.Client`,
   or a `curl` subprocess.
3. For each, confirm: (a) the trigger, (b) the request payload, (c) the auth, (d)
   whether the user can turn it off.

If you find an outbound call that is **not** in this list — or one that fires under
conditions different from what's documented — **please file a bug**. We treat the
"no telemetry" promise as a load-bearing claim and want this doc to be the only
place where outbound endpoints are enumerated. See `docs/V2-PANEL.md` directive 14
for the v2 rationale.

---

## Priority items for the next sprint

These are flagged in the relevant tables above; consolidated here so they don't get
lost:

1. **Updater check at startup** (`updater.checkAsync()` from `main.zig`) should be
   opt-in or at least surface a one-time prompt. Today it pings GitHub on every
   launch with no setting to disable.
2. **yt-dlp first-launch download** from GitHub releases should show a prompt
   ("Opal wants to download yt-dlp ~30MB from github.com — Allow / Skip").
3. **Remote JSON API bind address.** Even with the v2 bearer token, default-binding
   to `0.0.0.0` exposes the API to the entire LAN. Consider `127.0.0.1` by default
   with an opt-in LAN toggle.
4. **`extractors.zig` `--cookies-from-browser=firefox`** is unconditional. Some
   users won't want their Firefox cookies surfaced to yt-dlp. Add a setting.
5. **Jellyfin `DeviceId="zigzag-001"`** is shared across all installs. Switch to a
   per-install random hex string stored in config — that's actually more private
   *and* better for Jellyfin's session list.
